programme.desc: A home invader ties Samantha up while kidnapping and murdering her husband; as Samantha explains what happened to her, it turns out that there may be more to this crime than there seems.

programme.title: Exile
programme.desc: Just prior to his release from prison, Ted Evans receives a threat from the man whose family he killed in a DUI. Believing the danger is real, Ted lives a reclusive life to protect his family. Starring Adam Beach, Camille Sullivan, Garry Chalk.

programme.title: Who Killed Our Father?
programme.desc: After the death of her foster sister, Leila takes a DNA test and discovers the identity of her biological father and sister. However, when her father is tragically murdered, she soon finds herself in more danger than she ever could have imagined. Starring Kirsten Comerford, Devin Cecchetto, Joanne Boland.

programme.title: Staged Killer
programme.desc: A popular TV morning show hosts life begins to fall apart when a former co-anchor returns. Starring Chrishell Hartley, Nicole Bilderback, Jason Dolley.

programme.title: Levels
programme.desc: In the future, a man watches in horror as his girlfriend gets murdered, and the killer seemingly disappears into midair. He soon embarks on a voyage of discovery that leads to shocking revelations that his reality is not what it appears to be. Starring Cara Gee, Peter Mooney, Aaron Abrams.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: A young police officer discovers the dark secret hidden in a neighborhood restaurant.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: When Erin Chorney disappears, suspicion falls on her ex-boyfriend Michael Bridges, the last person to have seen her; after investigators searches come up empty-handed, they orchestrate an operation to woo Bridges into confessing her whereabouts.

programme.title: One Way Mirror
programme.desc: After police discovers Melinda Pleskovic dead in her home just days before her daughters wedding, police turn to her future son-in-law when his story doesnt seem to add up; he has news of his own, break-ins at the Pleskovic home reveal the culprit.

programme.title: One Way Mirror
programme.desc: A home invader ties Samantha up while kidnapping and murdering her husband; as Samantha explains what happened to her, it turns out that there may be more to this crime than there seems.

programme.title: Exile
programme.desc: Just prior to his release from prison, Ted Evans receives a threat from the man whose family he killed in a DUI. Believing the danger is real, Ted lives a reclusive life to protect his family. Starring Adam Beach, Camille Sullivan, Garry Chalk.

programme.title: Robin Hood
programme.desc: A Saxon foresters son loses everything; a legend is born in blood; the fight for Englands soul begins.

programme.title: Take Cover
programme.desc: Trapped inside a penthouse with little to no cover, a professional sniper must battle an army of thugs to make it out alive. Starring Scott Adkins, Alice Eve, Billy Clements.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Jess is a woman on the run, desperate to cross the Canadian border to escape her past. On her tail is a resourceful agent whose life-altering diagnosis leaves her one step behind, with the stakes of her pursuit rising to apocalyptic proportions. Starring Sonya Walger, Hayley Erin, Tony Amendola.

programme.title: The Pope of Greenwich Village (1984)
programme.desc: Two cousins (Eric Roberts, Mickey Rourke) try to get rich quick with a racehorse and mob money in New Yorks Little Italy.

programme.title: 2 Days in the Valley (1996)
programme.desc: A hit man (Danny Aiello) falls for an art dealers helper (Glenne Headly) amid confusion in the San Fernando Valley.

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI trainee (Jodie Foster) seeks advice from a brilliant, psychopathic prisoner (Anthony Hopkins) to catch a killer who skins his victims.

programme.title: Hannibal (2001)
programme.desc: Seven years have passed since Dr. Hannibal Lecter (Anthony Hopkins) escaped from custody. The doctor is now at large in Europe. Mason Verger (Gary Oldman) remembers Lecter too, and is obsessed with revenge.

programme.title: Pet Sematary (1989)
programme.desc: A familys life in small-town Maine is shattered by the evil unleashed from an ancient American Indian burial ground.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: A gruesome discovery sends shockwaves through the town as Jade and Tabitha struggle with the weight of their revelation.

programme.title: Transformers One (2024)
programme.desc: Brothers-in-arms Orion Pax and D-16 become sworn enemies Optimus Prime and Megatron.

programme.title: 2 Days in the Valley (1996)
programme.desc: A hit man (Danny Aiello) falls for an art dealers helper (Glenne Headly) amid confusion in the San Fernando Valley.

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar (Cary Grant) sees fireworks with an American heiress (Grace Kelly) on the Riviera.

programme.title: Invasion of the Body Snatchers (1956)
programme.desc: A couple (Kevin McCarthy, Dana Wynter) discover that plant-life pods from space are replacing ordinary citizens.

programme.title: Beauty Shop (2005)
programme.desc: After a falling-out with an overbearing employer, reputed hairstylist Gina decides to go independent. Soon enough, she opens the doors of her new salon to an amusing crowd of patrons.

programme.title: The Fire Inside (2024)
programme.desc: The story of Claressa "T-Rex" Shields, a boxer from Flint, Mich., who trained to become the first woman in her countrys history to win an Olympic gold medal in the sport.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 (2024)
programme.desc: Sonic, Knuckles and Tails reunite to battle Shadow, a mysterious new enemy with powers unlike anything theyve faced before. With their abilities outmatched in every way, they seek out an unlikely alliance to stop Shadow and protect the planet.

programme.title: Waynes World (1992)
programme.desc: Wayne and Garth battle an evil TV producer in this big-screen adaptation of the "Saturday Night Live" skit.

programme.title: Waynes World 2 (1993)
programme.desc: The worlds best roadie helps goofy Wayne (Mike Myers) and Garth (Dana Carvey) organize a rock concert called Waynestock.

programme.title: Ferris Buellers Day Off (1986)
programme.desc: A cool teen (Matthew Broderick) plays hooky in Chicago with his girlfriend (Mia Sara), his buddy (Alan Ruck) and the classic Ferrari of his buddys father.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: A gruesome discovery sends shockwaves through the town as Jade and Tabitha struggle with the weight of their revelation.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Boyd tries to save Acosta from herself as Julie digs deeper into her newfound abilities; Tabitha embarks on a desperate gamble and Victor joins Ethan in a quest for answers.

programme.title: Daddys Home (2015)
programme.desc: An insecure man (Will Ferrell) competes for the affection of his stepchildren when their freewheeling father (Mark Wahlberg) breezes back into town.

programme.title: The Dictator (2012)
programme.desc: A North African tyrant (Sacha Baron Cohen) risks his life to ensure that democracy never comes to the country he so lovingly oppresses.

programme.title: Suburbicon (2017)
programme.desc: Suburbicon is a peaceful, idyllic, suburban community with affordable homes and manicured lawns. But the tranquil surface masks a disturbing reality, as family man Gardner Lodge navigates the towns dark underbelly of betrayal, deceit and violence.

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: A cattle baron (John Wayne) fights with his foster son (Montgomery Clift) on the first cattle drive up the Chisholm Trail.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Boyd tries to save Acosta from herself as Julie digs deeper into her newfound abilities; Tabitha embarks on a desperate gamble and Victor joins Ethan in a quest for answers.

programme.title: The Fortune Cookie (1966)
programme.desc: A TV cameraman hurt while covering a football game is told by his brother-in-law how to get a big insurance settlement.

programme.title: The Great Gatsby (1974)
programme.desc: A shady millionaire (Robert Redford) pines for another mans wife (Mia Farrow) amid the lavish parties and opulent mansions of 1920s Long Island.

programme.title: Shine a Light (2008)
programme.desc: Filmmaker Martin Scorsese documents a 2006 Rolling Stones concert at New Yorks Beacon Theatre, featuring guest appearances by Buddy Guy, Christina Aguilera and Jack White III.

programme.title: Blame It on Rio (1983)
programme.desc: A middle-aged coffee broker (Michael Caine) eyes his partners (Joseph Bologna) nubile teenage daughter (Michelle Johnson) on vacation in Brazil.

programme.title: Hoosiers (1986)
programme.desc: A college basketball coach (Gene Hackman) leaves the Navy in 1951 and becomes coach of an underdog Indiana high-school team.

programme.title: Capote (2005)
programme.desc: Writer Truman Capote (Philip Seymour Hoffman) researches the brutal murder of a Kansas family to pen the book "In Cold Blood."

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: Born with facial differences that, up until now, have prevented him from going to a mainstream school, Auggie Pullman becomes the most unlikely of heroes when he enters the local fifth grade.

programme.title: Almost Famous (2000)
programme.desc: An aspiring teenage rock journalist gets his big break when he follows an up-and-coming band on its tour.

programme.title: The Queen (2006)
programme.desc: Queen Elizabeth II and Prime Minister Tony Blair try to reach a compromise in determining the royal familys public reaction to Princess Dianas death.

programme.title: Rain Man (1988)
programme.desc: A wheeler-dealer (Tom Cruise) meets his brother (Dustin Hoffman), an institutionalized autistic-savant, heir to $3 million.

programme.title: Better Man (2024)
programme.desc: The true story of the meteoric rise, dramatic fall, and remarkable resurgence of British pop superstar Robbie Williams.

programme.title: The Train (1965)
programme.desc: A railroad boss (Burt Lancaster) helps the Resistance stop a Nazi colonel (Paul Scofield) from smuggling French art.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)
programme.desc: An outlaw (Henry Fonda) working for a railroad magnate fights a stranger (Charles Bronson) for a New Orleans widows (Claudia Cardinale) land.

programme.title: Baywatch (2017)
programme.desc: When a dangerous crime wave hits the beach, Mitch Buchannon leads his elite squad of lifeguards on a mission to prove that you dont have to wear a badge to save the bay.

programme.title: The Adventures of Buckaroo Banzai Across the Eighth Dimension (1984)
programme.sub-title: The Adventures of Buckaroo Banzai Across the Eighth Dimension (1984)
programme.desc: A space hero and his team of do-gooders battle Dr. Lizardo and his army of Red Lectroids.

programme.title: Mystic Pizza (1988)

programme.title: The Hunted (2003)
programme.desc: Aided by the FBI, a retired combat-trainer (Tommy Lee Jones) searches for a former student (Benicio del Toro) who is killing civilians in Oregon.

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: In an insurance scam, a man fakes his death and sets his wife up to take the blame. After serving 10 years in prison, she sets out to get revenge, immune from the law since she cannot be re-prosecuted for the same crime.

programme.title: Mercy (2026)
programme.desc: In the near future, an advanced AI judge tells a captive detective that hes on trial for the murder of his wife. If he fails to prove his innocence within 90 minutes, hell be executed on the spot.

programme.title: V for Vengeance (2022)
programme.desc: After learning that their younger sister escaped an earlier kidnapping attempt that also killed their parents, two estranged sisters must join forces to rescue her from a group of bloodthirsty vampires.

programme.title: The Contractor (2022)
programme.desc: When his new assignment goes awry, an elite soldier finds himself hunted and on the run, caught in a dangerous conspiracy and fighting to stay alive long enough to get home and uncover the true motives of those who betrayed him.

programme.title: Cheech and Chongs Last Movie (2024)
programme.desc: Cheech Marin and Tommy Chong discuss their lifelong friendship and popularity as a comedic duo. Features interviews, sketches and never-before-seen footage spanning their five-decade career.

programme.title: Grudge Match (2013)
programme.desc: A boxing promoter (Kevin Hart) offers two rival boxers (Robert De Niro, Sylvester Stallone) the chance to come out of retirement for one final bout.

programme.title: Jackass Presents: Bad Grandpa (2013)
programme.desc: Irving Zisman (Johnny Knoxville) takes his young and impressionable grandson (Jackson Nicoll) on a cross-country trip in which they encounter male strippers, biker-bar patrons and other unsuspecting citizens.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie (2002)
programme.desc: Nitwits (Johnny Knoxville, Bam Margera, Chris Pontius) perform outrageous stunts and pull practical jokes on an unsuspecting public.

programme.title: Jackass: Number Two (2006)
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his band of merry maniacs go on another madcap series of adventures involving outrageous stunts and downright craziness. With Bam Margera and Steve-O.

programme.title: Rumours (2024)
programme.desc: Leaders of the worlds wealthiest democracies become spectacles of incompetence, contending with increasingly surreal obstacles as night falls in the misty woods, and they realize they are suddenly alone.

programme.title: Top Secret! (1984)
programme.desc: A spoof of spy movies and Elvis Presley films, in which an American rock star gets caught up in high-level espionage.

programme.title: Mob Town (2019)
programme.desc: The FBI and a local cop track various members of organized crime families as they gather for a huge meeting in upstate New York in 1957.

programme.title: World Trade Center (2006)
programme.desc: Two Port Authority officers become trapped in the rubble of the World Trade Center after going in to help people escape from the buildings during the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001.

programme.title: Not Without Hope (2025)
programme.desc: Four friends face disaster when their boat capsizes in the Gulf of Mexico. Battling massive waves, sharks, dehydration and hypothermia, they cling to hope as the Coast Guard launches a daring rescue through a raging storm to save them.

programme.title: No Country for Old Men (2007)
programme.desc: An aging lawman (Tommy Lee Jones) reflects on his past and laments a changing world while trying to find and protect a hunter who took the cash from a drug deal gone bad.

programme.title: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
programme.desc: A World War II captain (Tom Hanks) and his squad (Edward Burns, Tom Sizemore) risk all to locate and send home a soldier whose three brothers died in combat.

programme.title: Day of the Fight (2023)
programme.desc: On the day of his first fight since his release from prison, a former boxing champion reconnects with his trainer, ex-wife, childhood friend, and abusive father in a quest for forgiveness and closure.

programme.title: Ali (2001)
programme.desc: Muhammad Ali (Will Smith) battles Sonny Liston, Joe Frazier and George Foreman and raises controversy outside the ring.

programme.title: Gangs of New York (2002)
programme.desc: During the era of Tammany Halls sway, a young man (Leonardo DiCaprio) vows vengeance on the vicious gangster (Daniel Day-Lewis) who killed his father.

programme.title: 68 Kill (2017)
programme.desc: A hardworking mans inability to say no to a beautiful women gets him in trouble when he agrees to steal $68,000.

programme.title: No Code of Conduct (1998)
programme.desc: Two policemen (Mark Dacascos, Charlie Sheen) learn that city leaders and law enforcement support heroin trafficking across the Mexican border.

programme.title: Run and Gun (2021)
programme.desc: A former criminal gets blackmailed into one last job to collect a mysterious package. After a deadly double-cross, he soon finds himself wounded and on the run from ruthless assassins who will stop at nothing to get what he has.

programme.title: The Time Machine (2002)
programme.desc: A scientist (Guy Pearce) travels into the future and meets a race of people who fear hideous monsters living under the ground.

programme.title: Jeff, Who Lives at Home (2011)
programme.desc: A 30-year-old slacker (Jason Segel) finds his true destiny while helping his brother (Ed Helms) stalk the latters possibly adulterous wife.

programme.title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.sub-title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.desc: A globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) must find Pandoras box before a maniacal scientist (Ciarán Hinds) can harness its power for world domination.

programme.title: Crisis (2021)
programme.desc: A drug trafficker organizes a smuggling operation while a recovering addict seeks the truth behind her sons disappearance.

programme.title: Scream (1996)
programme.desc: A psycho killer targets a past victims daughter (Neve Campbell), while a tabloid TV reporter (Courteney Cox) homes in on his identity.

programme.title: Scream 2 (1997)
programme.desc: A masked killer continues to pursue a California student (Neve Campbell) and her friends at an Ohio college.

programme.title: Scream 3 (2000)
programme.desc: Murders draw a young woman (Neve Campbell), a reporter (Courteney Cox Arquette) and an ex-policeman (David Arquette) to the set of a movie inspired by horrific events that they survived.

programme.title: Shell (2024)
programme.desc: Desperate to reclaim her career, once-beloved actress Samantha Lake gets drawn into the glamorous world of wellness mogul Zoe Shannon, only to uncover a monstrous truth beneath the seemingly flawless surface.

programme.title: Before We Vanish (2017)
programme.desc: Three aliens travel to Earth on a mission in preparation for a mass invasion. Having taken possession of human bodies, the visitors rob their hosts of the very essence of their being, leaving psychological and spiritual devastation in their wake.

programme.title: The Peacemaker (1997)
programme.desc: A scientist (Nicole Kidman) and an Army Special Forces colonel (George Clooney) travel the globe in pursuit of stolen nuclear weapons.

programme.title: Mindhunters (2004)
programme.desc: A serial killer targets a group of FBI trainees (Jonny Lee Miller, Kathryn Morris) and a Philadelphia policeman (LL Cool J) on a deserted island.

programme.title: Paycheck (2003)
programme.desc: After learning that his memory has been erased, a technical wizard (Ben Affleck) goes on the run to piece together clues from his past.

programme.title: Star Trek: First Contact (1996)
programme.desc: Picard (Patrick Stewart), Riker (Jonathan Frakes) and the others set off to stop the half-robot Borg from sabotaging the first warp drive flight in 2063.

programme.title: Star Trek: Section 31 (2025)
programme.desc: Emperor Philippa Georgiou faces the sins of her past after joining a secret division of Starfleet thats tasked with protecting the United Federation of Planets.

programme.title: Cypher (2002)
programme.desc: A man (Jeremy Northam) soon becomes confused about his own identity after he becomes a corporate spy for each of two rival firms.

programme.title: Walking Out (2017)
programme.desc: When a city teen travels to Montana to go hunting with his estranged father, the trip becomes a battle for survival when they encounter a grizzly bear.

programme.title: The Infernal Machine (2022)
programme.desc: Reclusive and controversial author Bruce Cogburn is drawn out of hiding when he begins to receive endless letters from an obsessive fan. What ensues is a dangerous labyrinth as Bruce searches for the person behind the cryptic messages.

programme.title: Ida Red (2021)
programme.desc: Crime boss Ida "Red" Walker turns to her son, Wyatt, to pull off one last heist to get out of prison. However, with the FBI closing in, Wyatt must soon choose between family and freedom.

programme.title: Snake Eyes (1998)
programme.desc: A corrupt detective (Nicolas Cage) and his Navy friend (Gary Sinise) probe a U.S. officials assassination at an Atlantic City boxing match.

programme.title: Scream 2 (1997)
programme.desc: A masked killer continues to pursue a California student (Neve Campbell) and her friends at an Ohio college.

programme.title: Scream 3 (2000)
programme.desc: Murders draw a young woman (Neve Campbell), a reporter (Courteney Cox Arquette) and an ex-policeman (David Arquette) to the set of a movie inspired by horrific events that they survived.

programme.title: Scream 4 (2011)
programme.desc: The Ghostface Killer returns with a vengeance when self-help author Sidney Prescott arrives in Woodsboro on the last stop of her book tour.

programme.title: Asking for It (2022)
programme.desc: A victim of sexual assault joins an all-female gang that targets violent frat boys and a corrupt police force of human traffickers.

programme.title: Backcountry (2014)
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bears territory.

programme.title: Miles to Go Before I Sleep
programme.desc: The relationships between musicians and their road crews and the importance of the people who work to put shows together.

programme.title: The Evening Star (1996)
programme.desc: A spunky woman (Shirley MacLaine) copes with her troubled adult grandchildren, feuds with an enemy and has a fling with a younger man (Bill Paxton).

programme.title: Alone in Berlin (2016)
programme.desc: A German couple lose their son in WWII, prompting them to become secretly anti-Nazi.

programme.title: Atlantic City (1980)
programme.desc: A boardwalk numbers runner (Burt Lancaster) feels young again with a casino clam shucker (Susan Sarandon) and a lucky cocaine deal.

programme.title: The First Wives Club (1996)
programme.desc: Three 50-ish college friends (Goldie Hawn, Bette Midler, Diane Keaton) plot revenge after their husbands dump them for younger women.

programme.title: The Back-up Plan (2010)
programme.desc: A single woman (Jennifer Lopez) meets the man (Alex OLoughlin) of her dreams on the same day she becomes pregnant through artificial insemination.

programme.title: The English Patient (1996)
programme.desc: A Hungarian counts (Ralph Fiennes) fling with a British newlywed leads to tragedy in World War II North Africa.

programme.title: Kate and Leopold (2001)
programme.desc: A Manhattan woman (Meg Ryan) falls in love with the 19th-century Duke of Albany (Hugh Jackman) who has stepped through a time portal.

programme.title: Muriels Wedding (1994)
programme.desc: An Australian social outcast (Toni Collette) moves to Sydney, cares for an ailing friend (Rachel Griffiths) and weds an Olympian needing citizen status.

programme.title: Florence Foster Jenkins (2016)
programme.desc: In 1940s New York, a man (Hugh Grant) goes to extreme lengths to make sure his wife (Meryl Streep) never finds out that shes an awful opera singer.

programme.title: Shall We Dance? (2004)
programme.desc: A married Chicago attorney (Richard Gere) takes dancing lessons with a beautiful woman (Jennifer Lopez) he saw through a window.

programme.title: Extraordinary Measures (2010)
programme.desc: After his two youngest children contract a fatal disease, a man (Brendan Fraser) joins forces with an unconventional scientist (Harrison Ford) in the hope of finding a cure.

programme.title: Clouds of Sils Maria (2014)
programme.desc: A veteran actress (Juliette Binoche) comes face-to-face with an uncomfortable reflection of herself when she agrees to take part in a revival of the play that launched her career 20 years earlier.

programme.title: The Back-up Plan (2010)
programme.desc: A single woman (Jennifer Lopez) meets the man (Alex OLoughlin) of her dreams on the same day she becomes pregnant through artificial insemination.

programme.title: View From the Top (2003)
programme.desc: A woman (Gwyneth Paltrow) from a small Nevada town makes friends while training to become a flight attendant.

programme.title: Failure to Launch (2006)
programme.desc: The parents of a young man (Matthew McConaughey) who still lives at home hire a beautiful woman (Sarah Jessica Parker) to entice him to finally leave the nest.

programme.title: Rosemarys Baby (1968)
programme.desc: A pregnant womans (Mia Farrow) husband (John Cassavetes) and their satanic neighbors want her baby to be the Antichrist.

programme.title: Last Weekend (2014)
programme.desc: Petty squabbling marks the Memorial Day reunion of an affluent matriarch (Patricia Clarkson) and her dysfunctional family (Chris Mulkey, Zachary Booth).

programme.title: Losing Isaiah (1995)
programme.desc: An ex-crack addict (Halle Berry) fights for custody of her son adopted by a social worker (Jessica Lange) and her husband (David Strathairn).

programme.title: Bridget Joness Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Befuddled mom-to-be Bridget Jones (Renee Zellweger) must figure out which of the two men (Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey) in her life is the father of her child.

programme.title: Bride and Prejudice (2004)
programme.desc: Mrs Bakshi is looking for suitable husbands for her daughters. When the rich single gents Balraj and Darcy visit, the Bakshis have high hopes, but circumstances get in the way of romance.

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to stay in the country, a gay man proposes a green card marriage to a female friend in exchange for paying for her IVF treatment. However, things get complicated when his grandmother surprises them with an extravagant Korean wedding banquet.

programme.title: A Single Man (2009)
programme.desc: Planning to commit suicide at the end of the day, a gay professor (Colin Firth) who recently lost his lover goes about his daily routine and visits with longtime friends.

programme.title: Stepping Out (1991)
programme.desc: A professional singer/dancer (Liza Minnelli) grooms her diverse amateur-dance class for a charity tap show.

programme.title: The Fan (1981)
programme.desc: Douglas, a record salesman, is an obsessive fan of actress Sally Ross. When his letters are rejected, he strikes out at her and her loved ones.

programme.title: The Sense of an Ending (2017)
programme.desc: A business owner (Jim Broadbent) reunites with his first love (Charlotte Rampling) after a letter and a diary force him to confront the past.

programme.title: Regarding Us (2025)
programme.desc: After losing her teaching career, A transgender woman has a profound impact on the lives of two children.

programme.title: The Best of Me (2014)
programme.desc: The funeral of a close friend reunites former high-school sweethearts, who find that they are still in love after 20 years apart.

programme.title: Mommie Dearest (1981)
programme.desc: Movie queen Joan Crawford (Faye Dunaway) pampers and punishes her adopted son and daughter (Diana Scarwid).

programme.title: Cursed (2005)
programme.desc: Siblings (Christina Ricci, Jesse Eisenberg) hunt for the werewolf that attacked them and transformed them into lycanthropes.

programme.title: True Grit (2010)
programme.desc: A teenage girl (Hailee Steinfeld) enlists the aid of a boozy, trigger-happy lawman (Jeff Bridges) to hunt down her fathers killer (Josh Brolin).

programme.title: Fatal Attraction (1987)
programme.desc: A New York lawyer (Michael Douglas) with a wife (Anne Archer) and daughter sleeps with a client (Glenn Close) whose lust turns to hate.
